title: The Macro Minute: Is POTUS correct to push back on Fed tightening?

Happy Monday out there team 42. It's your skipper here Darius Dell to present our macro minute for Monday June 8th 2026. As always we'll start with the executive summary from today's leadoff morning note. So let's dive right in. Today's key macro question is is pus correct to push back on Fed tightening? The short answer is no. Both the market and the data are telling him he's wrong. Terms of analytical nuance. Our market implied Fed neutral rate model now sees the Fed as two rate hikes behind the curve which means the Fed must hike twice to remove its increasingly accommodative bias. Three orthogonal vectors are combining right now to signal to members of the FOMC that they must tighten to combat [clears throat] the buildup of core inflationary pressure in the US economy. Number one, the massive aggregate demand shock from AIAPEX is combining with proyclical fiscal stimulus from the one big beautiful bill. Number two, the sharp slowdowns in labor supply and productivity growth suggest the risk of the economy overheating from a neighbor and output gap perspective is high. And number three, the monetary drivers of inflation are nearing dangerous tipping points that may begin to spiral if the Fed commits to easy money. Most importantly, none of these vectors has anything to do with the USIsrael ran conflict, which means the risk of the Fed targeting the wealth effect channel to re in price pressures this summer is much higher than currently assumed by the median market participant. Refer to our June6 around the horn webcast titled, "Is it time to party like it's 1998?" for more details. Transition to our 42 macro dashboard is always wrap up with a question from our community. This one's titled uh best macro skying report yet. says, "Love the improvement to the slideshow and your uh macrocotting report video presentation was the best shed skipper. Wonder if you have any so thank you for the feedback. Wonder if you have any opinion as to how bitcoins store a value will fare versus stocks in this higher growth, profitability, retaining earnings environment that typically would benefit stocks. I'm grateful for KISS and know you are continually back testing like you did recently for the gold allocation. Skipper, I've been a member for several years and your professional growth is a blessing to watch. Love conquers all. Amen, my friend. Appreciate that. So just getting on to the Bitcoin question specifically, recall that in terms of KISS, you know, there's a few elements of KISS that I think are important to to highlight here. Uh so KISS is built to counter uh the 6040 portfolio uh as a as a as a long-term wealth uh uh generator uh and retirement uh uh strategy for for the median investor. And so uh you know, one of the things that KISS does is to offensively expose our portfolios to productivity growth. That's the 60% target allocation to stocks. It defensively protects our portfolios from financial oppression and monetary debasement. Financial oppression being the 30% target allocation to gold and the 10% target allocation to Bitcoin defensively protects our portfolios from financial oppression. We have long since exited the prior regime, the great moderation regime where the key risk in financial markets was deflation was was a recession was deflation. we have since transitioned and I would argue starting in 2021 uh uh transition and jump condition to a different regime or the primary risk facing most investor portfolios is inflation. And so you have to instead of using bonds to protect uh for downside risk from a diversification standpoint, you must shift to assets that tend to do well when the central banks and governments around the world uh particularly here in the US economy are reaching for financial oppression and monetary debasement as solutions to their problems as opposed to uh what typically has been driven by the private sector which are uh uh credit cycle downturns, business cycle downturns, recession. And so again, that that transition is a massive transition that we think, you know, investors are largely aware of now, but have not made enough changes in their core asset allocations to essentially account for that. And so we're going to, in our opinion, continue to benefit long-term from flows uh out of fixed income into assets like uh gold and bitcoin uh as as more and more investors realize what we realized years ago when we were designing KISS that uh this just transition had taken place and that deflation was no longer the key risk in asset markets. inflation is now the key risk particularly in the context of public sector debt being larger than the total quantity of private sector debt around the world. So that's a that's a key thing to be aware of and so in kind of getting back into this question how will bitcoin Bitcoin is not designed from a portfolio construction standpoint to take advantage of productivity growth. That's what the stock allocation is doing and that's what stock allocation has been doing for us in case in terms of helping us compound returns in this equity bull market, this 42 macro paradigm C bull market which we got kickstarted for you back in April of last year. Um and so uh in terms of Bitcoin, you know, so right now we have a tremendous amount of monetary contraction around the world from the perspective of key central bank balance sheets. We also have key central banks around the world uh uh looking to tighten monetary policy has been priced into their four rate curves and bond markets in recent months. So it's no surprise to us at all to see uh Bitcoin and gold diverge from the productivity story uh in the equity market and the profitability story in the equity market. And this is exactly what portfolio construction is designed to do. It's designed to create diversification so you can mitigate the risk, the total risk in the portfolio while still taking advantage of the upside when there is upside to capture in stocks, gold and bitcoin. And obviously our systems, the the risk management overlays and kiss uh um you know have done a great job for us in terms of keeping us on the on the sidelines for this draw down, this most recent draw down in gold uh in Bitcoin. And so we're thankful for that as well. So just kind of summarizing, KISS plays offense with stocks, productivity growth is what you're trying to capture there. It plays defense with gold and Bitcoin and risk management overlays that feature ball targeting and dynamic position sizing. So again, you can't just you can't just sit there in a static 60/40 portfolio or 7030 portfolio or whatever portfolio you you you you um your your adviser has you allocated to that has way too many bonds in it. Uh you know, we continue to pound that table and been have been right on that and we expect to continue to be right on that over the long term. So, we'll wrap it up there. Derry Still here presenting our macro minute for Monday, June 8th, 2026. Best of luck out there today. We'll catch you back here tomorrow. Go Nicks. Cheers.
